body_html:
no

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

To get 15 MM shares of LTD into The Family Trust's
Bear Stearns account, Peg
suggests that we transfer the following shares of
LHW held at the following
brokers:

4,756,037 shares from Merrill Lynch (all shares

in certificate form
bearing a restrictive
legend -- this could mean that an opinion
from Howard is required
and that Merrill will
have to talk to Sam)
5,827,638 shares from Smith Barney (all shares in
street name).
4,416,325 shares will be used from Bear Stearns (all
shares in street name).

All other LTD shares of LHW at Smith Barney and Bear
Stearns are in
certificate form bearing a restrictive legend; even
if we took all the certificated
shares from each of Smith Barney and Bear Stearns,
we would still have to take
certificated shares from Merrill. So, Peg decided
to take all the shares
from Merrill, which also empties LHW's Merrill Lynch
account of LTD shares.

After the transfers from LHW's Merrill Lynch and
Smith Barney Accounts to
LHW's Bear Stearns account and from LHW's Bear
Stearns account to the Bear
Stearns account of THe Family Trust : there will be
no shares remaining in LHW's
account at Merrill Lynch, 514,634 certificated
shares in LHW's account at
Smith Barney and 667,553 shares (of which 630,092
are certificated) in LHW's
account at Bear Stearns.

Is this acceptable? Please advise.

Date: Wed, 15 Mar 2006 13:49:07 -0500
To: dKIESQ@aol.com
From: Peg Ugland pegu@naproperty.com
Subject: transfer requests

Darren,
Attached are the requests to transfer the following:
4,756,037 shares from Merrill Lynch (all shares
in certificate)
5,827,638 shares from Smith Barney (all shares
in broker name)
4,416,325 shares will be used from Bear Stearns
(all shares in Broker name)

This leaves no shares at ML, 514,634(certificate)
shares at SB, and 667,553
shares at Bear Stearns (630,092 certificate shares).

Peg

http://mail.yahoo.com
